name: Slab Team Knowledge Base API
what_it_does: Slab team knowledge base REST API for engineering and product teams to create, organize, and search company documentation with Unified Search across connected tools and AI-powered knowledge retrieval. Enables AI agents to manage post creation and topic organization for team knowledge automation, handle Unified Search across Slab, Slack, GitHub, Jira, Salesforce, and Notion for cross-tool knowledge retrieval automation, access AI-powered answer generation from team knowledge for question answering automation, retrieve document analytics and content performance tracking for knowledge effectiveness automation, manage topic and subtopic taxonomy for hierarchical knowledge organization automation, handle team member access and permission management for knowledge governance automation, access post verification and content status tracking for knowledge quality automation, retrieve integration connectivity to developer tools for engineering knowledge automation, manage template library for standardized documentation automation, and integrate Slab with Slack, GitHub, Jira, Notion, Salesforce, and Google Drive for end-to-end engineering team knowledge management.
use_cases: Building engineering wikis from developer knowledge agents
Searching across tools from unified knowledge retrieval agents
Answering team questions from AI knowledge assistant agents
Organizing company documentation from taxonomy management agents
not_for: Customer-facing help center and knowledge base (use Intercom Articles or Zendesk Guide)
Sales and support team verified knowledge (use Guru for expert-verified knowledge)
best_when: An engineering or product team wanting AI agents to automate knowledge base management, cross-tool Unified Search, AI-powered Q&A from team documentation, and topic-based knowledge organization through Slab's developer-friendly knowledge platform.
avoid_when: UNIFIED SEARCH REQUIRES CONNECTED TOOL INTEGRATIONS: Slab's Unified Search differentiator requires OAuth connections to Slack, GitHub, Jira, and other tools; automated knowledge retrieval via Unified Search depends on active tool integrations; automated knowledge workflows that don't establish tool connections get only Slab-native content without cross-tool search capability. TOPIC HIERARCHY DEPTH LIMITATION: Slab uses Topics (top-level) and nested subtopics for organization; maximum nesting depth may not accommodate deeply hierarchical enterprise documentation structure; automated content migration from deeply nested wikis must plan for Slab's organizational model constraints. POST vs UNIFIED SEARCH QUERY TYPES: Slab API distinguishes between post-level search (Slab content only) and Unified Search (cross-tool); automated knowledge retrieval must use correct search endpoint type; automated Q&A using post-level search when Unified Search is needed returns incomplete results missing Slack or GitHub context.

auth.notes: Slab uses OAuth 2.0 for API access with GraphQL API. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2016 by Jason Chen and Warren Wu. Backed by Craft Ventures, Matrix Partners ($12M+ raised). Products: Team knowledge base, Unified Search (cross-tool), AI answers, Topics organization, integrations. Integrations: Slack, GitHub, Jira, Notion, Salesforce, Google Drive, Asana, Confluence. GDPR. SOC2. Serves engineering and product teams. Competes with Guru, Notion, and Confluence for team knowledge management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free up to 10 users (Starter plan) with unlimited posts
pricing.notes: San Francisco CA. Craft Ventures/Matrix backed. Free Starter (10 users). Per-user subscription. Annual billing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; GraphQL query complexity limits; webhooks for post create, update, and delete events

known_agent_gotchas: GRAPHQL QUERY COMPLEXITY LIMITS FOR DEEP QUERIES: Slab uses GraphQL API with query complexity scoring; automated knowledge retrieval with deeply nested queries (fetching posts with all related topics and verifications in one query) may exceed complexity limits; automated knowledge export must paginate and limit query depth; batch fetching patterns required for large knowledge base operations

UNIFIED SEARCH REQUIRES ACTIVE TOOL OAUTH CONNECTIONS: Slab's Unified Search across Slack, GitHub, and other tools requires each tool OAuth connection to be active and authorized; automated knowledge retrieval via Unified Search must verify all required tool connections are active; automated search that expects cross-tool results without verifying tool connections returns Slab-only results without error

POST PERMISSIONS INHERITANCE FROM TOPIC: Slab post visibility inherits from Topic permissions (private, team, or workspace); automated post creation must set Topic with correct permission level for intended audience; automated knowledge creation in private Topic creates posts invisible to intended audience if Topic scope is wrong

WEBHOOK PAYLOAD CONTENT vs FULL POST FETCH: Slab webhooks include post ID and metadata but not full post content; automated knowledge sync workflows receiving post-update webhooks must make separate GraphQL query for updated content; automated content mirroring based on webhook payload alone creates incomplete content sync

AI ANSWERS FEATURE CREDIT CONSUMPTION BY PLAN: Slab's AI answer generation from team knowledge consumes monthly credits on Business plan; automated AI Q&A via Slab must monitor credit consumption; automated high-frequency question answering for chatbot integration creates unexpected credit depletion; implement AI answer query budgeting per subscription tier
agent_readiness.security_notes: Team knowledge base. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US. Team documentation and search data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Knowledge base. SOC2, GDPR. OAuth2. US. Team documentation and search data.
